questions.text: Williamsons synthesis is used for the synthesis of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols are reacted with metallic sodium to form alkoxides. This alkoxide ion is a strong nucleophile and readily reacts with alkyl halide to produce an ether.

questions.text: Aldehydes after catalytic reduction change to:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones on reduction with hydrogen in the presence of a metal catalyst like Pd, Pt or Ni form primary and secondary alcohols respectively. Hydrogen is added across the carbonyl group.

questions.text: When ether is protonated, the conjugate acid formed is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: When ethers are protonated, the resulting conjugate acid is called oxonium ion and it has a poitive charge.

questions.text: Which of the following alcohol is commonly used as anti-freeze:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ethylene glycol is a dihydric alcohol and it is used as anti-freezing agent.

questions.text: Which of the following will have the highest boiling point:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: "2-hexanone has more surface area, which is why it has a high boiling point, as it has more bonds to be broken down. So, its boiling point is high."

questions.text: 95% ethanol is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohol obtained by fermentation is only upto 12% and never exceeds 14% because beyond this limit enzymes become inactive. This alcohol is distilled again and again to obtain 95% alcohol which is called rectified spirit. Absolute alcohol can aioo be obtained by redistillation.

questions.text: Which of the following alcohol is used in the perfumes and for flavouring:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Perfume alcohol typically consists of ethanol (ethyl alcohol) as the primary base. Ethanol is also used in flavouring.

questions.text: According to Lewis concept ethers behaves as:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In ethers, on oxygen atom there are two lone pairs of electrons and hence ether acts as Lewis base.

questions.text: Which enzyme is not involved in fermentation of starch:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The enzymes involved in the fermentation of starch are: diastase, maltase and zymase. Urease is responsible for catalyzing the hydrolysis of urea into carbon dioxide and ammonia

questions.text: The hydrolysis of sugar is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hydrolysis of sucrose to give glucose and fructose is called inversion. hydrolysis of sucrose brings about a change in the sign of rotation, from dextro (+) to laevo (–) and the product is named as invert sugar.

questions.text: . Which compound shows maximum hydrogen bonding with water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Lower alcohols are generally colourless toxic liquids with characteristic sweet smell and burning taste They are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. So alcohols with greater number of carbons will have less hydrogen bonding with water as compared to lower alcohols.

questions.text: Which statement is incorrect about phenol:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: It is colourless, crystalline poisonous solid
It does not turn blue litmus paper red
It liberates CO2 gas from carbonate
Above 65.9°C it is miscible wither water

questions.explanation: Phenol is much more acidic than alcohols but less acidic than carboxylic acids. It dissolves readily in alkalies but it is too weak to affect the litmus paper or to evolve CO2 from carbonates. Its dissociation constant (Ka ) is 1.3xl0-10

questions.text: Which of the following is the weakest acid:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Relative acidic strength of alcohol, phenol, water and carboxylic acid is as follows:Carboxylic Acids>Phenol>Water>Alcohols The acidic strength of a substance depends on the stability of the conjugate base. Stable is the conjugate base, stronger is the acid. The phenoxide ion is very stable because of resonance whereas in case of ethanol, there is no resonance and the +I effect of the ethyl group increase the electron density on the oxygen atom and destabilize the system. So, alcohol is weakest acid and carboxylic acid is the strongest acid.

questions.text: Which inorganic reagent may be used to distinguish between phenol and methanol:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Phenol is oxidised in the presence of K2Cr2O7/dil. H2SO4 to give oxidation product 1,4 benzo-quinone whereas methanol does not readily oxidise with this reagent.

questions.text: Phenol is also called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Aromatic compounds which contain one or more OH groups directly attached with carbon of benzene ring are called Phenols. The simplest example is phenol which is also known as Carbolic acid i.e. C6H5OH. It was first obtained from coaltar by Runge in 1834.

questions.text: . Which of the following give iodoform test:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ethanol gives iodoform with iodine in the presence of NaOH. Formation of yellow crystals indicate that the alcohol is ethanol. Methanol does not give iodoform test. Ethanol is the only primary alcohol to give the triiodomethane (iodoform) reaction. If "R" is a hydrocarbon group, then you have a secondary alcohol. Lots of secondary alcohols give this reaction, but those that do all have a methyl group attached to the carbon with the -OH group.

questions.text: H bonds break:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols react with other reagents in two ways (i) Reactions in which C — O bond breaks (ii) Reactions in which O — H bond breaks If a nucleophile attacks, it is the C — O bond which breaks. On the other hand, if an electrophile attacks on alcohol, it is the O — H bond which breaks. Which bond will break depends upon the nature of the attacking reagent.The order of reactivity of alcohols when O — H bond breaks:Primary alcohols>Secondary alcohols>Tertiary alcohols

questions.text: Which of the following alcohol is least soluble in water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Lower alcohols are generally colourless toxic liquids with characteristic sweet smell and burning taste They are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. So butanol will be least soluble in water.

questions.text: 100% pure alcohol is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohol obtained by fermentation is only upto 12% and never exceeds 14% because beyond this limit enzymes become inactive. This alcohol is distilled again and again to obtain 95% alcohol which is called rectified spirit. Absolute alcohol (100% alcohol) can also be obtained by redistillation of rectified spirit.

questions.text: The correct name of H2C = CH − CH2 − CH2 − OH is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The unsaturated alcohols are numbered in such a way that hydroxyl group rather than the point of unsaturation gets the lower number.

questions.text: Which of the following is more reactive:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Phenol shows two type of reactions. 1. Reactions due to — OH group 2. Reactions due to benzene ring Phenols are less reactive to nucleophiles so nucleophilic attack is less favoured, i.e - OH is not easy to replace while electrophilic attack on the ring is easy. The reactivity of compound towards electrophilic nitration depends on the electron density present on the compound. The higher the electron density, the more reactive will be the compound towards electrophilic nitration.Here, phenol is most reactive towards electrophilic reagent as thepresence of hydroxyl group induces electron donating effect which increases theelectron density.

questions.text: Bakelite is thermosetting plastic, it is formed by the polymerization of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Phenol reacts with formaldehyde (methanal) in the presence of acid or alkali to give hydroxy benzyl alcohol which on further reaction with other phenol molecules yield a polymer called bakelite.

questions.text: Which of the following compound should have lowest boiling point:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Melting and boiling points of alcohols are higher than corresponding alkanes. Methyl alcohol and ethyl alcohol are liquids while methane and ethane are gases. This is also due to hydrogen bonding which is present in alcohols but absent in alkanes. ethers are less polar than alkyl halides hence lower boiling point than alkyl halides but more than corresponding alkanes.The boiling point of organic molecules increases in the order: Alkanes > Alkenes > Alkynes > Haloalkanes > Ether > Ester > Amine > Aldehyde > Ketone > Alcohol > Carboxylic Acid > Amide.

questions.text: Ethanol can be converted into ethanoic acid by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols convert them into aldehydes and ketones. The best reagent for this purpose is acid dichromate. Further oxidation of these aldehydes/ketones yields carboxylic acids.

questions.text: When 2-pentanol undergoes oxidation the product is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols convert them into aldehydes and ketones. The best reagent for this purpose is acid dichromate. Primary alcohols yield aldehydes whereas secondary alcohols yields ketones. Tertiary alcohols resist oxidation. Hence, 2-pentanol on oxidation forms 2-pentanone.

questions.text: When ethyl bromide (C2H5Br) is heated with Ag2O in the abscence of moisture the product formed is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides are heated with dry silver oxide to form ethers

questions.text: Which of the following compound has no unsaturation:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: 1.Methanal has a formyl group in which carbon is doubly bonded to oxygen hence unsaturation is present.2.Methoxy methane has an ether group in which oxygen is present in the middle of alpha-carbons and has single bonds only so no unsaturation is present.3.Phenol has delocalised pi bonds.4.Benzoic acid has delocalised pi bonds as well as carboxyl group with unsaturation.

questions.text: The conversion of ethanol to ethene is an example of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols react with con. H2SO4 and give different products at different temperatures.

questions.text: Ethanol reacts with sodium metal to liberate:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ethanol reacts with sodium metal to produce sodium ethoxide and release one mole of hydrogen gas.

questions.text: Diethyl ether can be decomposed by heating with:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ethers are comparatively inert substances. The reagents like ammonia, alkalies, dilute acids and metallic sodium, have no action on ethers in cold state. Moreover, they are not oxidized or reduced easily. However ethers show some reactions, e.g. 1. With hydrogen iodide ethers give alcohols which can react further to give alkyl iodides.

questions.text: Which compound shows hydrogen bonding:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. Usually ethers are volatile liquids, highly inflammable with low boiling points. They are slightly soluble in water but freely soluble in organic solvents. Ether molecules do not show hydrogen bonding with one another but they show weak hydrogen bonding with water molecules due to which they are slightly soluble in water.Alkyl halides do not show hydrogen bonding and neither do alkanes.

questions.text: Which compound shows maximum hydrogen bonding with water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. Ethers generally do not show hydrogen bonding with water, or if so, then very weak bonding that is negligible.

questions.text: Which compound is more soluble in water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. Ethers are less soluble.

questions.text: Which compound is more soluble in water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohols are readily soluble in water but solubility decreases in higher alcohols. The solubility of alcohols is due to hydrogen bonding which is prominent in lower alcohols but diminishes in higher alcohols. Ethers are less soluble.

questions.text: Ethanol can be converted into ethanoic acid by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols convert them into aldehydes and ketones. The best reagent for this purpose is acid dichromate. Further oxidation produces carboxylic acids.

questions.text: Which enzyme is not involved in fermentation:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Enzymes involved in fermentation are: diastase, maltase, zymase. Urease is not used in fermentation.

questions.text: Which compound is called a universal solvent:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Water is called the "universal solvent" because it is capable of dissolving more substances than any other liquid. This is important to every living thing on earth.

questions.text: Methyl alcohol is not used:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Methanol is used as a solvent for fats oils, paints, varnishes. It isalso used as antifreeze in the radiators of automobiles and fordenaturing of alcohol.Ethanol is used as a solvent, as a drink and as a fuel insome countries. Moreover it. is used in pharmaceuticalpreparations and as a preservative for biological specimen.

questions.text: Rectified spirit contains alcohol about:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alcohol obtained by fermentation is only upto 12% and never exceeds14% because beyond this limit enzymes become inactive. This alcoholis distilled again and again to obtain 95% alcohol which is calledrectified spirit. Absolute alcohol can aioo be obtained by redistillationof rectified spirit in the presence of CaO which absorbs its moisture.

questions.text: According to Lewis concept ethers behave as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Acid as well as a base

questions.explanation: Ethers serve as Lewis bases. For instance, diethyl ether forms a complex with boron trifluoride, i.e. borane diethyl etherate
